_doc: Single source of truth for the fork -> upstream mergeback boundary. Consumed by tools/classify-diff.mjs and tools/detect-contract-change.mjs. See docs/mergeback-pipeline.md. This file is itself generic product and flows to forks via 'git merge upstream/main'.
_config_note: Everything under assets/data/ is excluded from upstream proposals: club identity/content seeds (club*.json, news/events/site/trainingszeiten.json) AND vendored domain seeds (age-classes, weight-classes, membership-types, demo-data). The CANONICAL domain seeds live in packages/api-contract/data/ (matched by packages/** -> generic), so seed improvements are proposed there, never via the vendored assets/data/ copy. theme.*.css and assets/img/** are the branding layer (incl. the neutral theme.example.css template); structural CSS lives in assets/css/styles.css (generic). .github/workflows/** is also config-classified (excluded from proposals): the Mergeback-Bot GitHub App lacks the 'workflows' permission and may not push workflow files, so workflow changes are handled by humans; the rest of .github/** stays generic.

assets/js/club-config.js.note: Generic resolver infrastructure, BUT entries inside the 'var EXAMPLES = [ ... ]' array are club-specific config. Additions inside the array are dropped; resolver-logic changes outside it are proposed; a single hunk straddling both is ENTANGLED (needs_human).

_forbidden_note: Catches leaked club IDENTITY in otherwise-generic files. Theme colors are NOT listed here on purpose: they cannot be enumerated per fork, and they are already structurally excluded because theme.*.css is config. The neutral baseline uses a handle-less instagram URL (https://www.instagram.com/), so 'instagram.com/<handle>' reliably means a real club handle. Patterns are matched case-insensitively.
_replacements_note: Applied by the deterministic --neutralize pass (and by the workflow's sed step) to scrub residual identity from generic files. Ordered most-specific first. Values are derived from neutral_baseline (club.example.json). The claude-code-action pass catches semantic identity that these regexes miss.
